category_title: Deaf and hard of hearing children with ASD who exhibit escape-motivated tantrums during difficult tasks
minimal_context: Charlie is sitting at a table in his classroom during reading class, and the teacher presents him with a book to read a paragraph.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Charlie, it is time for reading class. Please look at this book and read the first paragraph.

STUDENT: (Shakes his head and pushes the reading material away, refusing to work.)

TUTOR: We need to finish this reading lesson.

STUDENT: (Turns his body away from the table, continuing to show avoidance behaviors toward the difficult work assignment.)

TUTOR: Please turn around and read the paragraph.

STUDENT: (Screams, pounds his fist on the table, and leaves his chair to avoid the reading work.)

options.A: Instruct Charlie to take out a sheet of paper, write the book's title, and write as much as he can recall from beginning to end, reminding him it is a retelling, not a summary.
options.B: Purposefully select reading books and texts written by and/or featuring people of color to present to Charlie during the reading class when he is asked to read a paragraph.
options.C: Allow Charlie to select a positive reinforcer to work for before beginning the reading lesson, ensuring he earns that reinforcer when he finishes the paragraph or makes an honest attempt at it.
options.D: Devote one of the classroom's bulletin boards to reading book recommendations, keeping recommendation slips always available for Charlie to use during the reading class when he is asked to read a paragraph.
